items.title: Address observation issues (8 findings)
items.description:
- Contains 61% of project files (80/131) — may be too broad, consider splitting
- Perfect zone cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling indicates excellent internal architecture and external interface design
- Security guard system provides multi-layered protection with configurable policies for safe autonomous operation
- Dual provider architecture (CLI + API) ensures fallback options and different execution contexts
- Perfect zone cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling demonstrates excellent encapsulation of Claude API concerns
- Token usage tracking includes comprehensive cost estimation and budget enforcement mechanisms
- All zones achieve perfect cohesion metrics, suggesting the community detection algorithm successfully identified natural architectural boundaries
- No cross-zone imports found, indicating excellent architectural discipline and proper abstraction boundaries

items.title: Address observation issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Clean dependency inversion through claude-client shared foundation prevents circular dependencies between domain packages
- Perfect architectural isolation with no circular dependencies enables independent development and testing of each subsystem

items.title: Address observation issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Size distribution reflects natural complexity: foundation (15 files), domain (~65 files each), orchestration (114 files)
- Standardization debt exists across package exports, naming conventions, and test directory structures

items.title: Address observation issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Standardization debt exists across package exports, naming conventions, and test directory structures

items.title: Address observation issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Error hierarchy should be standardized: domain CLIError classes should extend foundation ClaudeClientError for consistent error handling patterns
- Process execution abstraction missing: scattered execFile imports across multiple zones should use centralized process management layer

items.title: Address observation issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts receives calls from 30 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects

items.title: Address observation issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts receives calls from 32 files (utility module — high fan-in expected) — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (12 findings)
items.description:
- Type imports from Rex (PRDStore, PRDItem, ItemStatus) create compile-time coupling despite dynamic runtime imports - the zone boundary is violated at the type level even though runtime coupling is avoided
- Cross-zone type imports create compile-time coupling that defeats runtime decoupling strategies - zones appear independent at runtime but are tightly coupled during TypeScript compilation
- PRIORITY_ORDER constants duplicated across multiple zones with inconsistent typing - shared constants should be extracted to a common location to prevent type drift and maintenance burden
- PRIORITY_ORDER constant duplicated with different type signatures (Record<Priority,number> vs Record<string,number>) across core/canonical.ts, core/next-task.ts, and analyze/propose.ts creating type safety gaps and maintenance burden
- Schema index.ts barrel export creates hidden bidirectional coupling by re-exporting from both viewer zone (v1.ts) and test-suite zone (validate.ts) - every analyzer importing schema creates transitive dependencies across three zones
- Direct import of schema/validate.ts from test-suite zone violates zone boundaries and forces Zod bundling into browser code where validation could be optional
- schema/validate.ts is misplaced in test-suite zone despite being imported by production CLI and viewer code - this file should be in analysis-engine zone based on its actual usage patterns
- Architecture creates false dichotomy between coupling and duplication - core domain types like Priority could be shared without compromising zone independence by treating them as stable contracts rather than implementation details
- Architecture forces choice between runtime coupling and maintenance burden - current solution chooses duplication over dependency, creating divergence risk that outweighs benefits of runtime independence for core domain types
- Architecture prioritizes runtime independence over type safety and maintainability - the systematic duplication of Priority and PRIORITY_ORDER across 5 locations creates a maintenance nightmare that outweighs any benefits of zero dependencies
- Systematic duplication of business logic constants (PRIORITY_ORDER) across 4 locations with inconsistent typing creates multiple sources of truth that risk diverging during maintenance - refactor to single shared constant in packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts and import consistently
- Type system fragmentation where identical Priority union is defined in both packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts:7 and packages/sourcevision/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/types.ts:17 - consolidate to single exported type to prevent drift and ensure consistency

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Hench maintains 14 direct imports from rex package across 9 files, creating controlled but extensive runtime dependency on PRD operations beyond just type contracts

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Inconsistent public API standardization — hench exports only schema types while rex and sourcevision provide comprehensive runtime function exports
- Configuration export inconsistency breaks consumer expectations - hench exports DEFAULT_CONFIG while rex/sourcevision hide defaults internally, forcing consumers to use different integration patterns per package

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Test architecture inconsistency: packages implement different test directory structures (unit|integration|e2e vs unit-only) despite documented standardization requirements
- Type duplication anti-pattern: web package manually duplicates rex types instead of shared interface abstraction, violating DRY and creating maintenance burden

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Architectural tension between zero-coupling goals and practical implementation needs forces systematic code duplication in interface layer

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Process execution scattered across 40+ files despite centralized abstraction existing in hench zone — exec.ts should be promoted to foundation layer

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (4 findings)
items.description:
- God modules at architectural boundaries (enrich.ts 925 lines, MCP server 400 lines) create maintenance bottlenecks and violate single responsibility principle
- Multiple packages duplicate utility functions (basename extraction, tree traversal, data preparation) instead of using shared abstractions or gateway pattern consistently
- Dead code accumulation in integration layers: 39+19+9+8+7+5+4=91 unused exports concentrated in adapter, validation, and UI component modules indicates systematic over-engineering at system boundaries
- God function: analyzeZones in packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts calls 88 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Domain logic leakage across package boundaries - complex rex business logic (epic stats, merge operations, tree utilities) is duplicated/embedded in web package route handlers instead of being properly abstracted in rex domain layer
- God function: CallGraphView in packages/web/src/viewer/views/call-graph.ts calls 63 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions
- God function: cmdAnalyze in packages/rex/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 52 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Domain package fragmentation: Rex (packages-rex:rex-cli) and sourcevision (packages-sourcevision:cli, packages-sourcevision:tests) are split across separate zones instead of being unified domain boundaries, creating artificial separation within business domains
- God function: ZoneDiagram in packages/web/src/viewer/views/zones.ts calls 67 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions
- God function: cmdAnalyze in packages/sourcevision/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 47 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions

items.title: Address suggestion issues (5 findings)
items.description:
- Extract PRIORITY_ORDER constant to shared location with consistent typing - currently duplicated in 4 locations with inconsistent types (typed Priority vs untyped string Record)
- Create shared foundation package at packages/shared/src/types.ts containing Priority union and PRIORITY_ORDER constant, then refactor packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts:7, packages/sourcevision/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/types.ts:17, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/prd-integration.ts, and packages/hench/src/core/types.ts to import from single source
- Consider decomposing hench zone into functional subzones: CLI interface (packages/hench/src/cli/), core agent logic (packages/hench/src/core/), and tool integrations (packages/hench/src/tools/) to improve maintainability of the 80-file zone
- Implement Priority type import in packages/hench/src/core/types.ts:1 by adding 'import { Priority } from "../../../rex/src/schema/v1.js"' and removing the duplicate union definition on line 3
- Refactor packages/hench/src/core/types.ts to import Priority from packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts instead of duplicating the union type definition

items.title: Address suggestion issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Execute standardization in dependency order: first complete hench public.ts exports (packages/hench/src/public.ts), then migrate test directories (packages/hench/__tests__ → packages/hench/tests/, packages/web/src/__tests__ → packages/web/tests/), finally establish monorepo naming convention
- Create packages/hench/src/public.ts exporting AgentConfig, RunConfig, RunResult, ExecutionEngine and update packages/hench/package.json exports field to include './public': './dist/public.js'
- Move packages/web/src/__tests__ to packages/web/tests/ with domain subdirectories (dashboard/, mcp/, server/) to align with rex and sourcevision patterns

items.title: Address suggestion issues (4 findings)
items.description:
- Directory constants (REX_DIR, HENCH_DIR, SV_DIR) hardcoded with string literals instead of shared constants — creates silent drift risk when CLI and MCP implementations diverge
- Standardize hench public API exports to match rex/sourcevision pattern by exporting runtime functions alongside schema types in src/public.ts
- Extract hardcoded directory constants (REX_DIR, HENCH_DIR, SV_DIR) into claude-client foundation package as shared PROJECT_CONSTANTS export
- Standardize DEFAULT_CONFIG export pattern by adding to rex and sourcevision public APIs to match hench implementation

items.title: Address suggestion issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Process execution anti-pattern: scattered execFile imports across multiple modules instead of centralized process abstraction
- Error hierarchy should be standardized: domain CLIError classes should extend foundation ClaudeClientError for consistent error handling patterns
- Process execution abstraction missing: scattered execFile imports across multiple zones should use centralized process management layer

items.title: Address suggestion issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Promote process/exec.ts abstraction to claude-client foundation layer to eliminate process execution duplication across zones
- Process execution should be promoted from hench zone to foundation layer — exec patterns scattered across 40+ files indicate this belongs in claude-client for system-wide standardization
- Standardize process execution by promoting hench/src/process/exec.ts to claude-client foundation layer — currently scattered across 40+ files

items.title: Address suggestion issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Multiple viewer sub-zones across the codebase suggest need for UI component lifecycle and consolidation governance
- Direct child_process imports in routes-rex.ts should be refactored to use foundation layer spawnTool abstraction for architectural consistency
- Multiple viewer sub-zones suggest need for viewer consolidation strategy to prevent UI component fragmentation and maintenance burden

items.title: Address suggestion issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Consolidate duplicate tree utilities in web package by creating a shared internal module that both server/mcp-deps.ts and components can import from

items.title: Address suggestion issues (11 findings)
items.description:
- Console.log statements concentrated in orchestration files (86 occurrences) suggest debugging artifacts that should be replaced with structured logging
- Implement shared utility extraction: move basename extraction, tree traversal, and data preparation functions from packages/web, packages/rex, and packages/sourcevision into @n-dx/claude-client foundation to eliminate triplication
- Hub function: walkTree in packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts is called from 20 files — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed
- 39 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/viewer/graph/renderer.ts have no incoming calls: constructor, highlightNode, centerOnNode, selectNode, clearSelection (+34 more)
- 19 potentially unused exports in packages/rex/src/store/notion-adapter.ts have no incoming calls: constructor, path, getStatusGroupMap, invalidateCache, loadDocument (+14 more)
- 9 potentially unused exports in packages/hench/src/store/adjustments.ts have no incoming calls: loadAdaptiveStateAsync, saveAdaptiveStateAsync, recordAdjustment, updateSettings, lockKey (+4 more)
- 8 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/schema/validate.ts have no incoming calls: validateManifest, validateInventory, validateImports, validateZones, validateComponents (+3 more)
- 7 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/viewer/components/logos.ts have no incoming calls: NdxLogo, SourceVisionLogo, RexLogo, HenchLogo, NdxLogoPng (+2 more)
- 5 potentially unused exports in packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts have no incoming calls: isRequirementCategory, isValidationType, isPriority, isItemLevel, isItemStatus
- Extract shared tree traversal interface from core/tree.ts to reduce the 20-file dependency fan-out and improve testability
- Consolidate duplicate tree utilities into @n-dx/claude-client foundation package to eliminate redundancy across web, rex, and sourcevision packages

items.title: Address suggestion issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Hub function: info in packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts is called from 20 files — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed

items.title: Address suggestion issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Consider establishing maximum zone size policy to prevent over-aggregation (presentation-gateway at 159 files may exceed healthy boundaries)
- Zone size (159 files, 46% of codebase) suggests over-aggregation - consider splitting into web-ui, mcp-server, and api-gateway zones

items.title: Address pattern issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Perfect architectural metrics (cohesion=1, coupling=0) mask underlying design debt - zones achieve statistical perfection while creating practical maintenance burdens through forced duplication of shared business concepts
- Zone architecture achieves perfect runtime decoupling (no cross-zone imports) but creates maintenance burden through systematic type and constant duplication - suggests need for shared foundation package or reconsideration of zone boundaries for common business logic
- Zone architecture creates artificial boundaries that fragment natural business domain concepts - Priority and PRIORITY_ORDER are core domain concepts that transcend individual packages and should be centrally managed rather than duplicated

items.title: Address pattern issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Architecture follows clean dependency rules: orchestration→execution→domain→foundation with minimal controlled cross-coupling
- Standardization debt cluster: 5 related findings around package exports, naming conventions, and test structure indicate coordinated refactoring opportunity rather than independent fixes

items.title: Address pattern issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- Gateway pattern standardization — hench (ops.ts) and web (mcp-deps.ts) implement identical controlled coupling architecture
- Systematic standardization gaps across public APIs create integration friction despite sound architectural isolation - suggests need for package development guidelines

items.title: Address pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Systematic duplication pattern: zero-coupling architecture forces reimplementation of utilities across zones, violating DRY principle at system level

items.title: Address pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Domain layer isolation pattern allows rex and sourcevision to evolve independently while sharing foundation infrastructure

items.title: Address pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Process execution scattered across 40+ files indicates missing centralized process management abstraction

items.title: Address relationship issues (1 findings)
